summary: David's great song of deliverance and his last words are paired with Psalm 57, written when he fled from Saul in the cave — together they frame David's life as one of refuge found in God, steadfast praise rising from the depths of danger.
psalm_rows_html:
TypeAuthorSituationKey ThemeNotable VerseNT Connection
Lament / Praise (Miktam)DavidFleeing from Saul in the cave (1 Sam 22 or 24)Refuge in God amid mortal danger; praise rising from the pit to the heavensv. 7: "My heart is steadfast, O God, my heart is steadfast! I will sing and make melody!"v. 9 echoes Paul's praise among the Gentiles (Rom. 15:9); the refrain anticipates Christ's exaltation above all
